title: Mirror Symmetry for the Grassmannian and Its Schubert Varieties
speaker: Lauren Williams
abstract: While mirror symmetry for flag varieties and Grassmannians has been extensively studied, Schubert varieties in the Grassmannian are singular, and hence standard mirror symmetry statements are not well-defined. Nevertheless, in joint work with Konstanze Rietsch we introduce a ``superpotential'' for each Grassmannian Schubert variety, generalizing the Marsh-Rietsch superpotential for Grassmannians, and we show that it governs many toric degenerations of the Schubert variety. We also prove a ``polytopal mirror theorem'' for Schubert varieties: namely, for any cluster seed for the Schubert variety, we construct a corresponding Newton-Okounkov convex body, and show that it coincides with the superpotential polytope, that is, it is cut out by the inequalities obtained by tropicalizing an associated Laurent expansion of the superpotential. Finally, we observe that the lattice points of our Newton-Okounkov bodies have an interpretation in terms of quantum cohomology.
tags: Workshop on Combinatorics of Enumerative Geometry

proof_stages.label: Polytopal mirror ansatz beyond smooth Fano varieties
proof_stages.summary: The talk starts from Landau–Ginzburg mirror symmetry for smooth Fano varieties, recalls the Grassmannian equality between Newton–Okounkov bodies and superpotential polytopes, and promotes this equality as an ansatz that still makes sense for singular Schubert varieties.

proof_stages.label: Schubert opens and rectangle cluster charts
proof_stages.summary: Dual Grassmannians and their Schubert varieties are indexed by Young diagrams, open Schubert varieties are obtained by inverting distinguished rectangle Plücker coordinates, and plabic/cluster structures provide torus charts with rectangle variables.

proof_stages.label: Cluster valuations and Newton–Okounkov bodies
proof_stages.summary: Using a divisor indexed by P_λ and a chosen X-cluster chart, the speaker defines the valuation by leading terms of Plücker coordinates and obtains a Newton–Okounkov body that, for the rectangle chart, is a rational polytope identified up to coordinates with an order polytope.

proof_stages.label: Detropicalized Schubert superpotential W_λ
proof_stages.summary: The facet inequalities of the Schubert Newton–Okounkov body are detropicalized into Laurent monomials, and cluster relations rewrite their sum as a regular function on the open Schubert variety with denominators only along the frozen anticanonical boundary.

proof_stages.label: Lattice points and quantum cohomology shadow
proof_stages.summary: The lattice points of the Newton–Okounkov body are identified with valuations of nonvanishing Plücker coordinates, with coordinates given by a maximum-diagonal statistic that coincides with the Fulton–Woodward lowest q-degree in quantum Schubert multiplication.

proof_stages.label: Cluster-independent polytopal mirror theorem
proof_stages.summary: For any cluster seed, expanding W_λ in that seed and tropicalizing gives a superpotential polytope equal to the Newton–Okounkov body computed from the corresponding X-chart, providing the main polytopal mirror justification.

proof_stages.label: Toric mirror and root-polytope justification
proof_stages.summary: The Newton polytope of W_λ is shown to be reflexive and terminal, the associated Gorenstein Fano toric variety is related by a small partial resolution to the toric degeneration coming from the Newton–Okounkov body, and these properties are explained via general theorems on root polytopes of starred quivers.
